data.title: SPI Energy announces receipt of Nasdaq delist determination notice
data.description:
• SPI Energy (NASDAQ:SPI) on Wednesday said it has received a letter from Nasdaq, saying that as the company had not regained compliance with listing rules within the period as prescribed by the staff, its securities will be delisted from Nasdaq.
  • Separately, the letter also noted that the company did not meet the terms of the exception for demonstrating compliance with the listing rule, which requires the timely filing of all required periodic reports with the SEC.
  • The letter notified the company that it may appeal the Staff's determination to the Panel by October 22. The company has accordingly submitted a request for hearing before the panel.
  • SPI -0.62% after hours to $0.4431.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: IBM dips as Q3 2024 revenue falls short of market expectations
data.description:
IBM (NYSE:IBM) dipped 4% during post-market trading Wednesday after its third quarter 2024 financial results demonstrated revenue below consensus estimates.

For the quarter ended September 30, IBM reported adjusted earnings per share of $2.30, which was more than the consensus estimate of $2.23. However, total quarterly revenue of $14.97B fell short of the $15.08B estimate.

IBM's third quarter revenue represented a slight increase of 1% year over year.

"Heading into the final quarter of 2024, we expect fourth-quarter constant currency revenue growth to be consistent with the third quarter, with continued strength in Software," said IBM CEO Arvind Krishna. "We are confident in our ability to deliver more than $12 billion in free cash flow for the year, driven by continued expansion of our operating margins."

IBM's strongest segment growth during the third quarter came from software, which increased 9.7% year over year to $6.5B, which was more than the consensus estimate of $6.4B. Consulting segment revenue was $5.15B, a decrease of 0.5%. Infrastructure decreased 7% to $3.04B, and financing decreased 2.7% to $181M.

"Our investments are paying off in Software as we've repositioned our portfolio in recent years," said IBM Chief Financial Officer James Kavanaugh. "In the third quarter, Software delivered broad-based growth and now represents nearly 45 percent of our total revenue."

data.title: Veralto Non-GAAP EPS of $0.88 beats by $0.03, revenue of $1.31B in-line
data.description:
• Veralto press release (NYSE:VLTO): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.88 beats by $0.03.
  • Revenue of $1.31B (+4.0% Y/Y) in-line.
  • Guidance: For the fourth quarter of 2024, Veralto anticipates non-GAAP core sales growth in the low-to-mid single-digits year-over-year with adjusted operating profit margin of approximately 24.0% and adjusted diluted earnings per share in the range of $0.86 to $0.90 per share.
  • For the full year 2024, the Company expects core sales growth in the low single-digits with adjusted operating profit margin expansion of approximately 75 basis points. The Company raised its full year guidance for adjusted diluted earnings per share to a range of $3.44 to $3.48, up from its prior guidance of $3.37 to $3.45 per share. Free cash flow conversion guidance was maintained at a range of 100% to 110%.

data.title: Penske Automotive to acquire additional Porsche dealership in Melbourne
data.description:
• Penske Automotive Group (NYSE:PAG) on Wednesday said it has signed an agreement to acquire Porsche Centre Melbourne from Porsche Retail Group Australia Pty Ltd.
  • The acquisition is expected to add $130M in estimated annualized revenue, bringing the estimated annualized revenue for the three Porsche dealerships in Melbourne to $260M, the company said.
  • The acquisition is expected to close by the end of this year.
  • Source: Press Release
